title: schefflera littorea
intro: Schefflera littorea, also known as the sea umbrella, is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae, native to tropical regions of the Pacific and Indian Oceans. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and protecting against infections, due to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. Schefflera littorea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and has also been used in traditional remedies for skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
